summary: The installer progress screen now shows a shared ASCII-safe step-count bar derived from the existing step statuses, and fresh renderer/runtime/bootstrap verification passed on the current tree.

checks.name: shared-progress-surface
checks.detail: `renderProgressScreen()` now renders one aggregate progress bar for the entire installer flow instead of leaving long-running steps with only a local status label.

checks.name: terminal-step-accounting
checks.detail: Progress completion counts only `passed`, `warn`, `failed`, and `skipped`; `running` remains incomplete so the bar does not overstate progress.

checks.name: port-preflight-regression
checks.detail: The interactive port-preflight runtime harness now asserts that the rerun screen includes the aggregate bar while saving the chosen `HAPPYTG_MINIAPP_PORT` override.

checks.name: fresh-verification
checks.detail: `pnpm --filter @happytg/bootstrap build`, `pnpm --filter @happytg/bootstrap lint`, the targeted renderer test command, and the targeted runtime test command all exited 0 and are recorded in the task bundle.

checks.name: proof-bundle-completeness
checks.detail: The task bundle now includes the frozen spec, evidence, findings summary, verifier verdict, and raw command artifacts, and `task validate` reports the task as complete/passed.

residualRisks: The progress bar is step-count based rather than duration-based, so a single legitimately slow step can still hold the same completion ratio until it finishes.
